title: Sour Crumbly Lebanese Pork Stew
description: A tangy, pressurecooked Lebanese pork stew with a zesty lemontamarind splash, perfect for a quick keto-friendly meal that feels like comfort food on a sick day.
instructions: Cut pork shoulder into 2.5 cm cubes and season with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp cumin if desired.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a pressure cooker on sauté mode.
Brown the pork cubes in batches, 5–6 min per side, then set aside.
Add sliced onion to the cooker and sauté until translucent, about 3 min.
Stir in minced garlic and cook 30 sec.
Add tomato paste and cook 1 min, stirring to combine.
Pour in tomato puree, lemon juice, tamarind paste, and 500 ml water; stir well.
Return the pork to the pot, seal the pressure cooker, and cook on high pressure for 25 min.
Allow the cooker to release pressure naturally for 10 min, then quick‑release remaining pressure.
Stir the stew, adjust seasoning and add extra lemon juice if a stronger sourness is desired.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Enjoy your hearty, keto‑friendly Lebanese pork stew!
